claim: The blocker in atom ATOM_T005_S023_BLO_01 ("And the main issue is that it fails to reward exploration. So what do I mean ...") is dissolved by recognizing the first-principle in atom ATOM_T013_S025_FIR_01 ("But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in principle possible ...").
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The named blocker is an artifact of a frame that the first-principle invalidates; under the principle, the blocker either does not bind or is mis-stated.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the blocker (snippet S023) without invoking the dissolving principle (snippet S025); the connection is asserted by the analogy engine.
why_potentially_useful: A dissolved blocker often points to a previously-overlooked wedge: if the blocker was real, anyone who notices the dissolution gets a head start.
